title: malus × brevipes
intro: Malus × brevipes is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the rose family, produced by the crossing of Malus baccata and Malus communis. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Malus × brevipes include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Malus × brevipes can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
